review_summary: Textual grounding is sufficient for concept-focused questions, and no separate video clip is needed.

question_bank.question: In autoregressive generation, what happens after the model selects a token?
choices: The selected token is fed back as part of the context for later predictions.
The whole sequence is regenerated from scratch.
The model discards the previous context and samples independently.
The encoder must be retrained on the selected token.
question_bank.explanation: Autoregressive generation builds a sequence step by step. Each generated token becomes part of the prefix that conditions the next-token distribution.

question_bank.question: Why are decoder-only models commonly used for highly open-ended generation?
choices: They are a practical fit for left-to-right continuation over many possible outputs.
They guarantee the globally highest-probability sequence.
They avoid the need for a vocabulary.
They can only be used when an input sentence has a fixed translation.
question_bank.explanation: Open-ended generation often looks like continuing a prefix into many possible futures. Decoder-only autoregressive models naturally model this left-to-right continuation process.

question_bank.question: What is the key difference between teacher forcing and student forcing?
choices: Teacher forcing conditions on gold previous tokens during training, while student forcing conditions on the model's own generated tokens.
Teacher forcing samples randomly, while student forcing always chooses the gold token.
Teacher forcing is used only for evaluation, while student forcing is used only for data collection.
Teacher forcing removes the softmax layer, while student forcing adds it back.
question_bank.explanation: Teacher forcing supplies the correct previous tokens, making next-token learning easier. At generation time, the model no longer has those gold tokens and must condition on its own earlier outputs.

question_bank.question: What does “autoregressive” mean in left-to-right language generation?
choices: Each prediction is conditioned on previously generated tokens using the chain rule.
All tokens are generated simultaneously without any order.
The model predicts only the final token of a sentence.
The model conditions only on future tokens.
question_bank.explanation: Autoregressive generation decomposes a sequence probability into a product of next-token probabilities, each conditioned on the prefix produced so far.
